label: Homicide Rate Decline (2024–2025)
contestNote: Lowest homicide rate since 2016. Analysts caution official counts may undercount killings classified as accidents or disappearances. Post-Mencho CJNG fragmentation expected to reverse gains in 2026.
deltaNote: −34% vs. January 2025. Over half of all homicides in 7 states; Guanajuato most violent.

label: Registered Missing Persons
contestNote: Government registry figure; real total likely higher due to underreporting and reclassification. UN Committee found 'well-founded indications' of crimes against humanity.
deltaNote: 4,500+ clandestine graves with 6,200+ bodies and 4,600+ sets of remains discovered since 2006.

label: Days Since El Mencho Killed (CJNG Crisis)
deltaNote: CJNG succession crisis: Juan Carlos Valencia González consolidating control. O3 confirmed as field leader (El País, Apr 6). CJNG co-founder El 85 guilty plea Apr 7. CDN casinos sanctioned US Treasury Apr 14. Teotihuacan security overhaul Apr 22. Sheinbaum names economist Lazzeri as US ambassador (Apr 23). Ebrard corruption probe opened (Apr 24). US Ambassador warns bribery action imminent. USMCA formal round May 25.

label: World Cup Security Personnel Deployed
deltaNote: 20,000 military + 55,000 police + 2,500 vehicles + 24 aircraft across Guadalajara, Mexico City, Monterrey. Nationwide tourist site security overhaul ordered Apr 22. Counter-drone (CUAS) systems deployed at World Cup venues. CIA/Chihuahua diplomatic row adds security complexity.

label: Mexico Trade Surplus with US (2025)
deltaNote: Record US-Mexico trade surplus in 2025 fueling USMCA renegotiation pressure. 25% tariff applies to non-USMCA-qualifying goods. First formal USMCA bilateral round scheduled May 25, Mexico City.

label: Annual Cost of Violence to Economy
source: Vision of Humanity Mexico Peace Index — 2024
contestNote: Estimate equals ~18% of Mexican GDP; rose for first time since 2019 despite national homicide decline.

label: Population (2024)
deltaNote: Mexico grew from 25M (1950) to 130M (2024) — quintupling in 74 years. Urban population 81%; median age 29. Mexican diaspora in US: 36 million Mexican-Americans, 11M Mexican-born US residents.

label: Criminal Impunity Rate
contestNote: 97% of crimes in Mexico result in no conviction. Only 3% of crimes lead to any sentence. Mexico ranks 60th of 69 countries in the Global Impunity Index. Impunity is the structural enabler of organized crime.
delta: Only 1 in 33 crimes result in a conviction
deltaNote: SESNSP reports ~30,000 homicides/year; fewer than 1,500 annual homicide convictions — a 95% impunity rate for the most serious crime.
